{"key_event":"Mobutu’s one-party centralization","reasoning":"After the 1960-65 crisis, Mobutu restored central command over the army, provinces, parliament, courts, and key mineral revenues, raising state power from collapse/weakness to a moderate authoritarian capacity, though patrimonial corruption kept it below the colonial state’s earlier reach. Civil society fell sharply as parties, unions, student groups, professional bodies, elections, and even churches were subordinated or repressed under the MPR and Authenticité, leaving only limited residual resistance such as Catholic criticism.","society_change":-1.6,"society_power":2.4,"state_change":2.6,"state_power":5.8}
